title: Bank Transfer Race Condition
description: A bank transfer function that checks balance before deducting but lacks locking.
vuln_code:
def transfer(from_user, to_user, amount):
    balance = db.get_balance(from_user)
    if balance >= amount:
        # Simulate some processing delay
        time.sleep(0.1)
        db.update_balance(from_user, balance - amount)
        db.update_balance(to_user, db.get_balance(to_user) + amount)
        return True
    return False

stages.name: Find the vulnerability
stages.instruction: Identify why this code is vulnerable to race conditions.
stages.target: The check-then-act pattern (TOCTOU - Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use) is not atomic. Multiple threads could pass the balance check before the first one updates it.

stages.name: Exploit it
stages.instruction: Explain how an attacker would exploit this to send more money than they have.
stages.target: The attacker would send many simultaneous requests to transfer money. If the requests are processed in parallel, multiple requests might see the same initial balance and proceed with the transfer.

stages.name: Patch it
stages.instruction: How would you fix this in a SQL database using a single atomic update?
stages.target: UPDATE users SET balance = balance - ? WHERE id = ? AND balance >= ?

stages.name: Explain it
stages.instruction: Explain the concept of 'atomicity' in database transactions.
stages.target: Atomicity ensures that a series of operations are treated as a single unit, which either all succeed or all fail, preventing partial updates and race conditions.

hints: What happens if two requests come in at the exact same time?
Look at the time.sleep(0.1) - it increases the window of opportunity.
